overview: Mission Mausam is a flagship initiative of the India Meteorological Department (IMD) under the Ministry of Earth Sciences, aimed at enhancing weather forecasting, climate services, and disaster preparedness through advanced observational networks, numerical weather prediction models, and specialized services for agriculture, aviation, hydrology, and public safety. The scheme integrates satellite, radar, and surface-based observations to provide real-time weather data, forecasts, and warnings across India. It supports sectors such as power, water resources, and health by delivering sector-specific meteorological advisories and early warning systems.
objectives: Improve monsoon forecasts across all time scales from short-range to seasonal
Enhance observational infrastructure including Doppler Weather Radars, automatic weather stations, and satellite data reception
Develop and operationalize high-resolution numerical weather prediction models
Provide sector-specific services for agriculture, aviation, hydrology, and environmental monitoring
Strengthen early warning systems for extreme weather events such as cyclones, floods, heatwaves, and thunderstorms
Support climate research and services through data sharing and international collaboration
Enable real-time dissemination of weather information via digital platforms and mobile advisories
Build capacity through training programs for meteorological personnel and stakeholders
eligibility: The scheme is implemented by the India Meteorological Department under the Ministry of Earth Sciences and serves government agencies, public institutions, and end-users across sectors such as agriculture, aviation, power, water resources, and disaster management. Direct beneficiaries include farmers receiving agromet advisories, aviation authorities using meteorological watch offices, power grid operators utilizing weather portals for grid management, and state and central agencies accessing hydrometeorological and environmental data. There is no formal application process for end-users as services are disseminated publicly through IMD’s portals, SMS advisories, and bulletins.
benefits: Mission Mausam provides real-time weather forecasts, nowcasts, and extended range forecasts; sector-specific advisories such as Gramin Krishi Mausam Seva (GKMS) for farmers, aviation meteorological services for flight safety, hydrometeorological support for flood forecasting, and environmental monitoring including air quality and ozone services. It delivers early warnings for cyclones, thunderstorms, heatwaves, and flash floods through SMS, web portals, and mobile applications. The scheme enables secure and economic operation of the Indian power grid via weather-informed scheduling, supports agricultural planning with crop-specific advisories, and enhances disaster preparedness through impact-based forecasting. Benefits also include access to climate data, research collaboration, and training programs for capacity building.
financial_support: Mission Mausam is a centrally funded government scheme under the Ministry of Earth Sciences. Financial support is provided through budgetary allocations for infrastructure development, including procurement of Doppler Weather Radars (DWRs), automatic weather stations (AWS), high-performance computing systems for numerical weather prediction, and satellite data reception systems. Funds are utilized for upgrading observational networks, developing forecast models, operationalizing early warning systems, and maintaining service delivery mechanisms. The scheme does not involve direct financial transfers to individuals or entities but invests in national infrastructure and service capabilities.
application_process: Mission Mausam does not have a direct application process for end-users as it is a public service initiative. Services are automatically available to beneficiaries through IMD’s official channels. Farmers receive agromet advisories via SMS on registered mobile numbers through the mKisan portal. Aviation users access meteorological data through Aerodrome Meteorological Offices and Meteorological Watch Offices. Power sector users utilize the Weather Power Portal developed in collaboration with POSOCO. General public and agencies can access forecasts, warnings, and data through the IMD website (mausam.imd.gov.in), mobile apps like UMANG, and district-wise warning GIS portals. No formal application or registration is required for accessing general weather services.
deadlines: Rolling basis — services are available year-round and updated in real time
implementing_agency: India Meteorological Department (IMD), Ministry of Earth Sciences, Government of India

caveats: Services are dependent on observational data availability and model accuracy
Forecast accuracy decreases with increasing lead period
Action should be taken based on ORANGE and RED colour warnings
Vulnerable regions such as urban and hilly areas should prioritize heavy rainfall warnings
Some specialized services (e.g., DWR network) may be under maintenance
Access to certain data products may require registration or authorization
